title: Ctenodon lorentzianus
taxonomy: Ctenodon lorentzianus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenodon, which is a part of the order Malpighiales. The plant is native to New Caledonia, a region in the Pacific Ocean. The classification of Ctenodon lorentzianus is still being studied and refined by botanists. Further research is needed to determine the exact relationships within the plant's family and order.
uses: Ctenodon lorentzianus, also known as the Lorentz's ctenodon or Lorentz's ctenodonthus, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia. Unfortunately, there is limited information available on its medicinal uses. However, it is a member of the Rutaceae family, which includes many plants known for their medicinal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential health benefits of Ctenodon lorentzianus.
